channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hayley Wickenheiser leaves Maple Leafs after eight seasons in front office role
item.description: Hayley Wickenheiser, a hockey legend with a storied career, has parted ways with the Toronto Maple Leafs after eight seasons in the team's front office. The decision, announced by new general manager ...

item.title: Exploring the Complex Realities of Motherhood in Bollywood's New Play
item.description: Bollywood actor Kalki Koechlin and theatre director Sheena Khalid's play 'Belly of the Beast' confronts the often-overlooked challenges of motherhood, from postpartum depression and miscarriage to soc...

item.title: France defeat Morocco 2-0, reach World Cup semi-finals with Mbappe's goal
item.description: France secured a 2-0 victory over Morocco in their World Cup quarter-final clash at Boston Stadium, advancing to the semi-finals. Kylian Mbappe, the star striker, recovered from a missed penalty in th...

item.title: Melbourne's Last Miznon Restaurant Closes After Nearly a Decade
item.description: The Israeli street-food chain Miznon has announced the closure of its Melbourne CBD branch, marking the end of an almost 10-year chapter for the city's only remaining location. The decision comes afte...

item.title: BSNL's Satellite Phone for Specialized Use: Features and Purchase Guidelines
item.description: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) has introduced a satellite phone priced at ₹1.34 lakh, targeting specialized sectors like defense, maritime operations, disaster response, and remote areas. The dev...

item.title: Uganda Government Supports Repatriated Citizens with National IDs and Reintegration Programmes
item.description: The Ugandan government is moving swiftly to issue National Identity Cards to over 700 citizens who were recently repatriated from South Africa following xenophobic attacks. This initiative, led by the...

item.title: Online Sports Betting's Human Toll and Other News Stories
item.description: The article highlights the growing societal impact of online sports betting, with Ontarians spending $100 billion annually, leading to increased suicides and bankruptcies. It also covers the Shopify C...

item.title: Ubisoft Announces 'Just Dance Decades of Hits' Track List for September Release
item.description: Ubisoft has opted to announce its upcoming Just Dance game, titled 'Decades of Hits', with minimal details despite its October release date. The game focuses on popular songs spanning multiple decades...

item.title: Oracle's Credit Rating Downgrade Reflects Financial Concerns
item.description: Oracle Corporation's credit rating was downgraded by major agencies, signaling concerns about its financial health and market position. The downgrade comes amid rising interest rates and increased com...

item.title: Syria arrests suspects linked to Damascus bombings during Macron's visit
item.description: Syria's authorities have arrested several suspects accused of orchestrating recent bombings in Damascus, including the attacks that occurred during French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to the coun...

item.title: Spar recalls yoghurt products due to production fault affecting quality
item.description: Consumers in South Africa have been advised to avoid specific Spar yoghurt products following a nationwide safety recall triggered by a production fault. The National Consumer Commission (NCC) confirm...

item.title: TESDA Enhances Security of Skills Passport with New Identity Verification Measures
item.description: The Technical Education and Skills Development Authority (TESDA) has introduced a new electronic Know-Your-Customer (eKYC) feature in its TESDA Skills Passport to strengthen identity verification for ...

item.title: The Nerve Invites Organizations to Share Experiences on AI and Disinformation Impact
item.description: The Nerve, a data forensics company specializing in combating disinformation, is inviting professionals from brands, corporate communications, and PR to participate in an online interview. This initia...

item.title: Cruz Hewitt progresses to junior Wimbledon semi-finals after quarter-final victory
item.description: Cruz Hewitt, a young tennis prodigy, has made it to the semifinals of the junior Wimbledon tournament following his decisive quarter-final win. The match, which took place on Thursday night, showcased...

item.title: Tanzania trains water experts to protect small reservoirs
item.description: Water experts across Tanzania have received specialized training to monitor and protect water quality in small reservoirs, part of a national effort to ensure safe and sustainable water supplies for l...

item.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 lack crossplay between PS5 and PS4
item.description: The article discusses the absence of crossplay functionality in the newly released versions of Call of Duty: Black Ops and Black Ops 2 on PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4. Despite months of anticipatio...

item.title: Ukraine's potential missile manufacturing deal with US faces production hurdles and geopolitical complexities
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced a proposal to grant Ukraine the capability to manufacture Patriot interceptor missiles, a critical defense asset against Russian attacks. While this move could ...

item.title: Government rescues 160 trafficking victims, 57 arrested in anti-trafficking efforts
item.description: The Tanzanian government has rescued 160 citizens trafficked for illegal and exploitative labor, including sexual exploitation, between July 2025 and February 2026. The victims comprised 96 women, fou...

item.title: Mike Collins Faces Staff Shake-Up and Criticism Amid Senate Race
item.description: U.S. Senator Mike Collins (R-GA) is experiencing a significant staff shake-up as he loses his second congressional chief of staff, forcing him to open a third hiring process. According to The Atlanta ...

item.title: Teen Rider Arrested After Attempting to Escape Police in E-Motorcycle Pursuit
item.description: A 15-year-old boy on an electric motorcycle attempted to evade police in downtown Palm Springs by performing dangerous stunts such as wheelies and doughnuts, according to authorities. The incident occ...
